Klasse SyxParamInfo

java.lang.Object
de.btu.kt.syx.midi.SyxParamInfo
Alle implementierten Schnittstellen:
Serializable
public class SyxParamInfo extends Object implements Serializable
Instances of this class provide additional information for parameters of SyxDataStructs.

Additional parameter information is optional. It facilitates

  • validation of parameter values,
  • conversion between
    • the MIDI value of the parameter as stored in the system exclusive message and
    • the model value of the parameter as displayed on the hardware instrument,
  • and data binding.

Additional parameter information comprises:

  • General information
    • parameter type: there are
      • raw parameters: without additional parameter information (the permissible MIDI value range is automatically derived from the SyxDataStruct's format specifier),
      • range parameters: permissible MIDI values are in the range [minMidiValue...maxMidiValue],
      • enumerated integers parameters: permissible MIDI values are listed in valueMap, and
      • enumerated names parameters: permissible MIDI values are listed in valueNamesMap.
    • parent: The SyxDataStruct containing the parameter
    • pname: The parameter name in the format specifier of the SyxDataStruct containing the parameter
    • UID: The unique parameter name in the SyxMessage
    • descr: The parameter name (as seen on hardware instrument)
  • MIDI value information
    • defMidiValue: The default MIDI value (used for parameter initialization)
    • minMidiValue: The smallest permissible MIDI value1)
    • maxMidiValue: The greatest permissible MIDI value1)
  • MIDI↔model value conversion information
    • modelValueOffset: Offset of the model value wrt. to the MIDI value (range parameters only)
    • valueMap: A list of permissible model values mapped to their respective MIDI values (enumerated integers parameters only)
    • valueNamesMap: A list of permissible model value names mapped to their respective MIDI values (enumerated names parameters only)
  • Parameter change message information
    • paramChangeMsgFormat: Format specifier of the system exclusive parameter change message (optional)

1) In case of enumerated integers and enumerated names parameters, there may be MIDI values in the range [minMidiValue...maxMidiValue] which are not permissible.

Siehe auch:
  • Serialisierte Form

    Konstruktoren
    Konstruktor
    Beschreibung
    SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, int[] midiValues, int[] modelValues, String descr)
    Creates a new parameter info for an enumerated values parameter.
    SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, int[] midiValues, String[] valueNames, String descr)
    Creates a new parameter info for an enumerated value names parameter.
    SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, int minMidiValue, int maxMidiValue, int modelValueOffset, String descr)
    Creates a new parameter info for a range parameter.
    SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, int minMidiValue, int maxMidiValue, String descr)
    Creates a new parameter info for a range parameter.
    SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, String descr)
    Creates a new generic range parameter info.
    SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, SequencedMap<Object,Integer> valueMap, String descr)
    Creates a new parameter info for an enumerated values or an enumerated value names parameter.

  • Konstruktordetails

    • SyxParamInfo

      public SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, String descr) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Creates a new generic range parameter info.

      Notes:

      • Other constructors must invoke this constructor!
      • The smallest permissible MIDI value will be set to 0.
      • The largest permissible MIDI value will be set to the greatest possible value that can be stored for this parameter in parent.
      • The default MIDI value will be set to 0.
      • The model value offset will be set to 0.

      Parameter:
      parent - The SyxDataStruct containing the parameter
      pname - The parameter name in the SyxDataStruct containing the parameter: 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      UID - The unique parameter name in the SyxMessage
      descr - The parameter name (as seen on hardware instrument)
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if
      • parent is null
      • pname is invalid or no such parameter exists in parent
      • UID is null, empty, or invalid
      Siehe auch:
      • setValueRange(int, int, int)
      • setValueMap(SequencedMap)
      • setValueMap(int[], int[])
      • setValueMap(int[], String[])

    • SyxParamInfo

      public SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, int minMidiValue, int maxMidiValue, int modelValueOffset, String descr) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a new parameter info for a range parameter.

      This constructor invokes SyxParamInfo(parent,pname,UID,descr), and then setValueRange (minMidiValue,maxMidiValue,modelValueOffset).

      Parameter:
      parent - The SyxDataStruct containing the parameter
      pname - The parameter name in the SyxDataStruct containing the parameter: 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      UID - The unique parameter name in the SyxMessage
      minMidiValue - The smallest permissible MIDI value, non-negative
      maxMidiValue - The largest permissible MIDI value, non-negative
      modelValueOffset - The model value offset
      descr - The parameter name (as seen on hardware instrument)
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - for conditions see SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct,char,String, String) and setValueRange(int,int,int)
      InvalidMidiDataException - ditto

    • SyxParamInfo

      public SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, int minMidiValue, int maxMidiValue, String descr) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a new parameter info for a range parameter. Convenience shortcut for SyxParamInfo (parent,pname,UID,minMidiValue,maxMidiValue,0,descr).

      Parameter:
      parent - The SyxDataStruct containing the parameter
      pname - The parameter name in the SyxDataStruct containing the parameter: 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      UID - The unique parameter name in the SyxMessage
      minMidiValue - The smallest permissible MIDI value, non-negative
      maxMidiValue - The largest permissible MIDI value, non-negative
      descr - The parameter name (as seen on hardware instrument)
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if
      • parent is null
      • pname is invalid or no such parameter exists in parent
      • UID is null, empty, or invalid
      • minMidiValue>maxMidiValue
      InvalidMidiDataException - if
      • minMidiValue or maxMidiValue is negative
      • minMidiValue or maxMidiValue is greater than the greatest possible value that can be stored for this parameter in the SyxDataStruct that contains it

    • SyxParamInfo

      public SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, SequencedMap<Object,Integer> valueMap, String descr) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a new parameter info for an enumerated values or an enumerated value names parameter.

      This constructor invokes SyxParamInfo(parent,pname,UID,descr), and then setValueMap(valueMap).

      Parameter:
      parent - The SyxDataStruct containing the parameter
      pname - The parameter name in the SyxDataStruct containing the parameter: 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      UID - The unique parameter name in the SyxMessage
      valueMap - Map of model values to MIDI values, either
      • <Integer,Integer>: The key set contains all permissible model values and the value set contains the respective permissible MIDI values, or
      • <String,Integer>: The key set contains all permissible model value names (as seen on hardware instrument) and the value set contains the respective permissible MIDI values.
      descr - The parameter name (as seen on hardware instrument)
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - for conditions see SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct,char,String, String) and setValueMap(SequencedMap)
      InvalidMidiDataException - ditto

    • SyxParamInfo

      public SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, int[] midiValues, int[] modelValues, String descr) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a new parameter info for an enumerated values parameter.

      This constructor invokes SyxParamInfo(parent,pname,UID,descr), and then setValueMap(midiValues,modelValues).

      Parameter:
      parent - The SyxDataStruct containing the parameter
      pname - The parameter name in the SyxDataStruct containing the parameter: 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      UID - The unique parameter name in the SyxMessage
      midiValues - An array of permissible MIDI values
      modelValues - An array containing the respective model parameters for all permissible midiValues. If null, the constructor will use midiValues creating an identity map.
      descr - The parameter name (as seen on hardware instrument)
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - for conditions see SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct,char,String, String) and setValueMap(int[], int[])
      InvalidMidiDataException - ditto

    • SyxParamInfo

      public SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct parent, char pname, String UID, int[] midiValues, String[] valueNames, String descr) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a new parameter info for an enumerated value names parameter.

      This constructor invokes SyxParamInfo(parent,pname,UID,descr), and then setValueMap(midiValues,valueNames).

      Parameter:
      parent - The SyxDataStruct containing the parameter
      pname - The parameter name in the SyxDataStruct containing the parameter: 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      UID - The unique parameter name in the SyxMessage
      midiValues - An array of permissible MIDI values
      valueNames - An array the respective permissible value names (as seen on hardware instrument)
      descr - The parameter name (as seen on hardware instrument)
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - for conditions see SyxParamInfo(SyxDataStruct,char,String, String) and setValueMap(int[], String[])
      InvalidMidiDataException - ditto

  • Methodendetails

    • setValueRange

      public final void setValueRange(int minMidiValue, int maxMidiValue, int modelValueOffset) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets the MIDI value range and the model value offset making this parameter a range parameter.

      Notes:

      • If a MIDI value map or a value names map is present, the method will clear them.
      • If the present default MIDI value is outside the range [minMidiValue,maxMidiValue], the method will set it to minMidiValue.

      Parameter:
      minMidiValue - The smallest permissible MIDI value, non-negative
      maxMidiValue - The largest permissible MIDI value, non-negative
      modelValueOffset - The model value offset
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if minMidiValue>maxMidiValue
      InvalidMidiDataException - if
      • minMidiValue or maxMidiValue is negative
      • minMidiValue or maxMidiValue is greater than the greatest possible value that can be stored for this parameter in the SyxDataStruct that contains it
      Siehe auch:
      • setValueMap(SequencedMap)
      • setValueMap(int[], int[])
      • setValueMap(int[], String[])

    • setValueMap

      public final void setValueMap(SequencedMap<Object,Integer> valueMap) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets the MIDI value map making this parameter either a enumerated values or a enumerated value names parameter, depending on the type of argument valueMap.

      Notes:

      • The method will automatically detect and set the smallest and greatest permissible MIDI values according to valueMap.values().
      • The method will set the model value offset to 0.
      • If the present default MIDI value is not contained in valueMap.values(), the method will set it to the first MIDI value in the map.

      Parameter:
      valueMap - Map of model values to MIDI values, either
      • <Integer,Integer>: The key set contains all permissible model values and the value set contains the respective permissible MIDI values, or
      • <String,Integer>: The key set contains all permissible model value names (as seen on hardware instrument) and the value set contains the respective permissible MIDI values.
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if valueMap is null, or contains keys of bad or inconsistent type, or contains less than two entries
      InvalidMidiDataException - if valueMap contains negative MIDI values or MIDI values greater than the greatest possible value that can be stored for this parameter in the SyxDataStruct that contains it
      Siehe auch:
      • setValueRange(int, int, int)
      • setValueMap(int[], int[])
      • setValueMap(int[], String[])

    • setValueMap

      public final void setValueMap(int[] midiValues, int[] modelValues) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets the model values to MIDI values map making this parameter a enumerated values parameter.

      Notes:

      • The method will automatically detect and set the smallest and greatest permissible MIDI values according to midiValues.
      • The method will set the model value offset to 0.
      • If the present default MIDI value is not contained in midiValues, the method will set it to midiValues[0].

      Parameter:
      midiValues - An array of permissible MIDI values
      modelValues - An array containing the respective model parameters for all permissible midiValues. If null, the method will use midiValues creating an identity map.
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if
      • midiValues is null or contains less than two values, or
      • modelValues.length!=midiValues.length, or
      • modelValues contains duplicate values
      InvalidMidiDataException - if
      • midiValues contains negative values, or
      • midiValues contains values greater than the greatest possible value that can be stored for this parameter in the SyxDataStruct that contains it
      Siehe auch:
      • setValueRange(int, int, int)
      • setValueMap(SequencedMap)
      • setValueMap(int[], String[])

    • setValueMap

      public final void setValueMap(int[] midiValues, String[] valueNames) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets the model value names to MIDI values map making this parameter a enumerated value names parameter.

      Notes:

      • The method will automatically detect and set the smallest and greatest permissible MIDI values according to midiValues.
      • The method will set the model value offset to 0.
      • If the present default MIDI value is not contained in midiValues, the method will set it to midiValues[0].

      Parameter:
      midiValues - An array of permissible MIDI values
      valueNames - An array the respective permissible value names (as seen on hardware instrument)
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if
      • midiValues is null or contains less than two values, or
      • valueNames is null or contains less than two values, or
      • valueNames.length!=midiValues.length, or
      • valueNames contains duplicate values
      InvalidMidiDataException - if
      • midiValues contains negative values, or
      • midiValues contains values greater than the greatest possible value that can be stored for this parameter in the SyxDataStruct that contains it
      Siehe auch:
      • setValueRange(int, int, int)
      • setValueMap(SequencedMap)
      • setValueMap(int[], int[])

    • getMidiValueRange

      public final int[] getMidiValueRange()
      Returns the MIDI value range.
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of two integers: [minMidiValue ,maxMidiValue]

      public final int getOffsValue()
      Returns the model value offset.

    • getMidiValues

      public final int[] getMidiValues()
      Returns an array of permissible MIDI values for enumerated parameters or null for range parameters. The returned array is a copy of the internal data.
      Siehe auch:
      • valueMap
      • valueNamesMap

    • getModelValueRange

      public final int[] getModelValueRange()
      Returns the model value range.
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of two integers containing the smallest and the largest permissible model value iff this parameter is
      • a range parameter, or
      • an enumerated integers parameter whose model values are consecutive
    In all other cases, the method returns null.
    Siehe auch:
    • getModelValues()
    • valueMap
    • modelValueRange

  • getModelValues

    public final int[] getModelValues()
    Returns the permissible model values.
    Gibt zurück:
    A array of integer model values; null for enumerated value names parameters.
    Siehe auch:
    • getModelValueRange()

  • getValueNames

    public final String[] getValueNames()
    Returns an array of names for all permissible MIDI values in case of enumerated value names parameters or null in case of enumerated integer values or range parameters. The returned array is a copy of the internal data.
    Siehe auch:
    • valueNamesMap

  • getParamChangeMsgFormat

    public final String getParamChangeMsgFormat()
    Returns the format specifier of the system exclusive parameter change message, may be null.

  • setDefaultMidiValue

    public final void setDefaultMidiValue(int defMidiValue) throws InvalidMidiDataException
    Sets the default MIDI value.

    Note: If the parameter has another than the default value range or a value map, they must be set before setting the default value. Otherwise

    Ungültige Referenz 
    validation
    may fail.

    Parameter:
    defMidiValue - The default MIDI value
    Löst aus:
    InvalidMidiDataException - if defMidiValue is not permissible
    Siehe auch:
    • setDefaultModelValue(String)
    • defMidiValue

  • setDefaultModelValue

    public final void setDefaultModelValue(int defModelValue) throws InvalidMidiDataException
    Sets the default MIDI value. Convenience shortcut for setDefaultMidiValue(model2Midi(defModelValue)).

    Note: If the parameter has another than the default value range or a value map, they must be set before setting the default value. Otherwise validation may fail.

    Parameter:
    defModelValue - The default model value
    Löst aus:
    InvalidMidiDataException - if defModelValue is not permissible
    Siehe auch:
    • setDefaultMidiValue(int)
    • setDefaultModelValue(String)
    • defMidiValue

  • setDefaultModelValue

    public final void setDefaultModelValue(String defModelValue) throws InvalidMidiDataException
    Sets the default MIDI value. Convenience shortcut for setDefaultMidiValue(model2Midi(defModelValue)).

    Note: If the parameter has another than the default value range or a value map, they must be set before setting the default value. Otherwise

    Ungültige Referenz 
    validation
    may fail.

    Parameter:
    defModelValue - The default model value
    Löst aus:
    InvalidMidiDataException - if defModelValue is not permissible
    Siehe auch:
    • setDefaultModelValue(int)
    • setDefaultMidiValue(int)
    • defMidiValue

  • setParamChangeMsgFormat

    public final void setParamChangeMsgFormat(String format)
    Sets the format specifier of the system exclusive parameter change message.
    Parameter:
    The - format specifier

  • validateMidiValue

    public void validateMidiValue(int midiValue, boolean onInit) throws InvalidMidiDataException
    Validates a MIDI value. If the MIDI value is permissible, the method will return normally. Otherwise the method will throw an InvalidMidiDataException.

    Notes:

    • The method is invoked
      • before the parameter value is set, reset or initialized, and
      • while the SyxDataStruct containing this parameter is validated
    • For range parameters, the method checks whether midiValue is in [minMidiValue, maxMidiValue]. For enumerated parameters, the method checks whether midiValue is contained in the value map or in the value names map, respectively.
    • Derived classes may override this method. They should invoke the base class implementation. If init is true, overrides should not check dependencies on other parameter values. This may fail because dependencies may not have been properly initialized yet.

    Parameter:
    midiValue - The MIDI value
    onInit - true if the parameter value is being reset or initialized, false if the parameter value is being set or during model validation
    Löst aus:
    InvalidMidiDataException - if midiValue is not permissible

  • validateDefMidiValue

    public void validateDefMidiValue(int defMidiValue) throws InvalidMidiDataException
    Validates a default MIDI value. If the default MIDI value is permissible, the method will return normally. Otherwise the method will throw an InvalidMidiDataException.

    Notes:

    • The method is invoked
      • while the SyxDataStruct containing this parameter is
        Ungültige Referenz 
        validated
        , and
      • after the default parameter value has been retrieved.
    • For range parameters, the method checks whether defMidiValue is in [minMidiValue, maxMidiValue]. For enumerated parameters, the method checks whether defMidiValue is contained in the value map or in the value names map, respectively.
    • Derived classes may override this method. They should invoke the base class implementation.

    Parameter:
    defMidiValue - The default MIDI value
    Löst aus:
    InvalidMidiDataException - if defaultMidiValue is not permissible

  • midi2Model

    public final int midi2Model(int midiValue) throws InvalidMidiDataException
    Converts a MIDI value to its model value.
    Parameter:
    midiValue - The MIDI value
    Gibt zurück:
    The respective model value
    Löst aus:
    InvalidMidiDataException - if midiValue is not permissible

  • midi2ModelAsString

    public final String midi2ModelAsString(int midiValue) throws InvalidMidiDataException
    Converts a MIDI value to a string representation of its model value. For enumerated names parameters, the method returns the value name. For all other parameter types, the method returns a string representation of the model value as obtained by midi2Model(midiValue).
    Parameter:
    midiValue - The MIDI value
    Gibt zurück:
    A string representation of the respective model value.
    Löst aus:
    InvalidMidiDataException - if midiValue is not permissible
    Siehe auch:
    • Ungültige Referenz 
      #valueNames

  • model2Midi

    public final int model2Midi(int modelValue) throws InvalidMidiDataException
    Converts a model value to its MIDI value.
    Parameter:
    modelValue - The model value
    Gibt zurück:
    The respective MIDI value
    Löst aus:
    InvalidMidiDataException - if modelValue is not permissible

  • model2Midi

    public final int model2Midi(String valueName) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
    Converts a string representation of a model value to its MIDI value. The MIDI value is obtained as follows:
    1. If valueName represents an integer, the method will return the respective MIDI value as obtained by model2Midi(Integer.valueof(valueName)).
    2. For enumerated names parameters, the method returns the MIDI value for valueName.
    3. If both of the above fail, the method will throw a InvalidMidiDataException.
    Parameter:
    valueName - A string representation of the model value
    Gibt zurück:
    The respective MIDI value
    Löst aus:
    IllegalArgumentException - if valueName is null or empty
    InvalidMidiDataException - if valueName is not permissible

  • prettyPrint

    public String prettyPrint(String linePrefix)
    Pretty-prints this parameter info to a string.
    Parameter:
    linePrefix - A prefix string for printed lines (not applied to the first line)
    Gibt zurück:
    The printed string

  • prettyPrintModelValueSet

    public String prettyPrintModelValueSet(String linePrefix, int lineLength)
    Pretty-prints the set of model values to a string.
    Parameter:
    linePrefix - A prefix string for printed lines (not applied to the first line)
    lineLength - The maximal print line length (excluding the prefix string)
    Gibt zurück:
    The printed string

  • checkUID

    public static void checkUID(String UID) throws IllegalArgumentException
    Checks if a unique parameter name is valid. Valid UIDs
    • cannot be null or empty, and
    • must contain only word characters and '.'
    Parameter:
    UID - The unique parameter name to check
    Löst aus:
    IllegalArgumentException - if UID is invalid
